name: Browser Extension Validator
description: Validate browser extension packages against Edge, Chrome, Firefox, and Safari store policies — plus your own custom rules. Runs 100% locally inside VS Code.
longDescription: A VS Code sidebar extension that validates .zip / .crx / .xpi packages and unpacked folders against the published developer policies for Microsoft Edge, Chrome Web Store, Firefox AMO, and Safari Web Extensions. Bundled policy catalogs cover 165+ policies across all four browsers, including real reported rejection reasons. Every check runs locally — no telemetry, no uploads.

featureGrid.title: 4 Browser Policy Catalogs
featureGrid.description: Bundled policies for Edge (56), Chrome (38), Firefox (40), and Safari (31) — seeded from each store's published developer guidelines.

featureGrid.title: Real Rejection Reasons
featureGrid.description: Goes beyond official policy text — includes reasons real review teams have cited for rejections (Blue Argon, Purple Agate, and more).

featureGrid.title: AI Auto-Fix Prompt
featureGrid.description: Generates a copy-pasteable prompt grouped by browser and policy ID, ready to drop into ChatGPT, Copilot, or Claude.

featureGrid.title: Custom Definitions
featureGrid.description: Capture failure messages from your own review rejections and run them alongside built-in policies.

featureGrid.title: Scheduled Policy Refresh
featureGrid.description: Hashes upstream policy pages and notifies you when a source change is detected. Configurable interval, or run on demand.

featureGrid.title: No Telemetry
featureGrid.description: Every check runs locally inside VS Code. No data is sent anywhere.

name: My Extension
description: Short description shown on the card (1-2 sentences).
longDescription: Full description shown in the detail view (2-4 sentences).
